title: World Bank Enterprise Surveys — Harmonized Indicators by Country (via World Bank Data360, dataset WB_ES)
frequency: irregular (survey rounds, roughly every 3-6 years per country; latest round per country marked LATEST_DATA=true)
units: mixed — percent of firms, index scores (1-7 or 0-100), median days/counts; see UNIT_MEASURE column per row and wbes_indicator_codes.json for the ~585 indicator codes (self-descriptive prefixes: BREADY=business readiness, FIN=finance, CORR=corruption, INFOR=informality, OBST=obstacles, REG=regulation, TAX=tax, GEND=gender, MGMT=management, PERF=performance, JOBS=employment, WK=workforce, TR=trade)
license_terms: World Bank Open Data terms (CC BY 4.0)
notes:
IRAN IS NOT COVERED: World Bank Enterprise Surveys have never surveyed Iran. Confirmed via three independent checks: (1) World Bank Microdata Library catalog (microdata.worldbank.org/index.php/catalog/enterprise_surveys) of 773 country-survey studies jumps directly from 'Iraq' to 'Ireland' with no Iran entry; (2) the enterprisesurveys.org 'exploreeconomies' economy-selector dropdown does not list Iran; (3) most authoritatively, the official World Bank Data360 API query https://data360api.worldbank.org/data360/data?DATABASE_ID=WB_ES&REF_AREA=IRN returned {"count": 0, "value": []} — zero records for Iran across all ~585 indicators and all years. Iran's exclusion is consistent with WBES's operational model, which since ~2005 has generally required in-country field access via a local implementing partner and government/statistical-office cooperation for its Business Pulse Survey/ICS methodology; Iran, like North Korea and a handful of other states, has no WBES survey round on record. This is a genuine, confirmed data gap, not a download failure.

What WAS retrieved: harmonized WBES indicator data (via the same World Bank Data360 platform that also hosts WEF_GCIHH) for all 15 assigned comparator countries, all of which DO have Enterprise Survey rounds on record — including several high-income countries (USA, DEU, FRA, GBR, ITA, NLD, SWE, ESP, PRT, GRC) that were surveyed under WBES's post-2015 expansion into OECD/EU economies, not just the traditional emerging-market focus. Each row is one indicator observation with SDMX-style dimensions: REF_AREA, INDICATOR, TIME_PERIOD, OBS_VALUE, UNIT_MEASURE, SEX/AGE/URBANISATION demographic breakdowns (mostly '_T' = total), COMP_BREAKDOWN_1/2/3 (often firm-size or sector splits, e.g. WB_ES_M_SECTOR_1), LATEST_DATA flag, OBS_STATUS. Downloaded by paginating the Data360 REST API (max page size 1000 rows) per ISO3 country code; no bulk CSV export was found on the Data360 site itself. wbes_indicator_codes.json lists all 585 indicator codes returned for this dataset (no separate label/description endpoint was found — codes are abbreviation-based, e.g. WB_ES_M_OBST15='obstacle to business, %firms identifying as biggest obstacle', WB_ES_S_FIN15='% of firms with a bank loan/line of credit'; consult enterprisesurveys.org indicator descriptions PDF for exact definitions if needed later). No Cloudflare/anti-bot blocking encountered on this API.

failures.reason: World Bank Enterprise Surveys has never conducted a survey round in Iran — confirmed zero records via official Data360 API (DATABASE_ID=WB_ES, REF_AREA=IRN returns count:0), corroborated by absence from the World Bank Microdata Library catalog and the enterprisesurveys.org economy selector.
